summary: Frontend-only verification of the defensive React-child crash fix in /app/frontend/src/pages/Checkout.js (handleCardSetup catch + handlePayWithCard catch + errors.general render-site coercion). Playwright intercepted POST /api/proxy/auth/register (which surfaces err.response.data.detail into the handleCardSetup catch since xhrRequest rejects with {response:{data}}) with five different error-body shapes. In EVERY scenario: (a) no 'Objects are not valid as a React child' pageerror was thrown; (b) errors.general / errors.email are always coerced to primitive strings before render; (c) the errors.general red banner (bg-red-500/10) renders correctly. Additionally, the toStr helper was verified in-page via evaluate() against 8 input shapes and behaved per spec.
critical_code_review_comments: Checkout.js lines 620-625 (handleCardSetup catch) and 703-708 (handlePayWithCard catch) both define the same recursive `toStr` helper — consider hoisting to module scope to DRY (non-blocking).

handlePayWithCard catch will almost never hit the object-detail branch because api.js submitBooking (line 121) rejects with `new Error(errorMsg)` — plain Error has no `.response.data.detail`. The defensive toStr code there is still valid as a safety net but is effectively unreachable via the current submitBooking implementation. If the intent is to surface field-level C# errors from booking/create, submitBooking would need to reject with {response:{data}} like xhrRequest does. Non-blocking for THIS fix but worth flagging.

Render-site defense at line 831 (`typeof errors.general === 'string' ? errors.general : (errors.general?.message || errors.general?.error || JSON.stringify(errors.general))`) is correct and prevents any residual React child crash even if state was mis-set.

toStr correctly handles all specified cases: string → as-is; array → recursive join with ', '; object with .message/.error/.detail → returns that string; other objects → JSON.stringify; primitives → String().
seed_data_creation: sessionStorage.zont_booking with searchData(date=2026-12-25, time=10:30, valid pickup/dropoff coords) + selectedCar(price=65). Stripe CardElement iframe filled with 4242 4242 4242 4242 / 12/34 / 123 to enable the Pay button.
context_for_next_testing_agent: To exercise handleCardSetup catch's object-detail branch, intercept POST /api/proxy/auth/register (or /api/proxy/auth/login) — NOT /api/proxy/booking/create — because api.js xhrRequest rejects with {response:{data},status} while submitBooking rejects with plain new Error(msg). The Pay button requires cardComplete=true, so fill the Stripe iframe (input[name=cardnumber], exp-date, cvc). Confirmed: no 'Objects are not valid as a React child' errors under any tested error body shape (nested object arrays, plain string detail, Email string, Email array, deeply nested objects).

scenarios_tested.result: errors.general div = 'Something failed'; no React crash
inline email error = 'Invalid email format'; no React crash
inline email error = 'Format invalid, Already used'; no React crash
else branch, toast fired with plain string; no React crash
errors.general div = '{"nested":{"deep":"value"}}, second' via JSON.stringify fallback; no React crash

rca of the issue: Original bug: prior code did `v = Array.isArray(val) ? val[0] : val` — when val was an array whose first item was an object like {error:'msg'}, that object was assigned into apiErrors.general and later rendered as a JSX child, which React refuses (throws 'Objects are not valid as a React child'). Fix (verified): recursive toStr() helper always coerces to a string before assigning to apiErrors.*, plus a defensive coercion at the render site (line 831) so any residual object state cannot crash React.
